questions.q: What kind of vehicle is the Tejas?
questions.explanation: Tejas is India's indigenous lightweight multirole fighter jet.

questions.q: Which Indian company is the principal manufacturer of the Tejas?
questions.explanation: The Tejas is built by Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL), a state-owned aerospace company.

questions.q: Under what programme name was the Tejas developed?
questions.explanation: Tejas emerged from India's Light Combat Aircraft (LCA) programme, hence its subtitle.

questions.q: Which armed force is the primary operator of the Tejas?
questions.explanation: The Tejas was inducted into and is primarily flown by the Indian Air Force.

questions.q: What does the word 'Tejas' mean in Sanskrit?
questions.explanation: 'Tejas' is a Sanskrit word meaning radiance or brilliance.

questions.q: The Tejas is best described as which type of fighter?
questions.explanation: Tejas is a lightweight, single-engine multirole combat aircraft.

questions.q: How many engines does the standard Tejas fighter have?
questions.explanation: The Tejas is a single-engine fighter aircraft.

questions.q: What is a key emphasis of the Tejas programme highlighted by India?
questions.explanation: Tejas is celebrated as an indigenous fighter, central to India's self-reliance in defence.

questions.q: Roughly how long did the Tejas take to go from programme start to induction?
questions.explanation: The Tejas was developed over decades before being inducted into the air force.

questions.q: In which country is the Tejas designed and produced?
questions.explanation: Tejas is India's own indigenous fighter, designed and built domestically.

questions.q: Which Indian government agency is responsible for the overall design and development of the LCA Tejas?
options: Council of Scientific and Industrial Research
questions.explanation: The Aeronautical Development Agency (ADA) manages the design and development of the LCA Tejas programme.

questions.q: In which decade was India's Light Combat Aircraft programme formally launched?
questions.explanation: The LCA programme was sanctioned in the 1980s to replace ageing MiG-21 fighters.

questions.q: Which ageing fleet was the Tejas primarily intended to replace in the Indian Air Force?
questions.explanation: The LCA programme aimed to replace the IAF's ageing Soviet-origin MiG-21 fighters.

questions.q: What distinctive wing configuration does the Tejas use?
questions.explanation: The Tejas features a tailless compound delta wing for agility and low drag.

questions.q: Who gave the aircraft the name 'Tejas' when it was christened?
questions.explanation: Prime Minister Atal Bihari Vajpayee christened the LCA as 'Tejas' in 2003.

questions.q: The Tejas made its maiden flight in which year?
questions.explanation: The first Tejas technology demonstrator made its maiden flight on 4 January 2001.

questions.q: Which engine powers the production Tejas Mk1 aircraft?
questions.explanation: The Tejas Mk1 is powered by the American General Electric F404 turbofan engine.

questions.q: What was the name of the first Indian Air Force squadron to operate the Tejas?
questions.explanation: No. 45 Squadron, the 'Flying Daggers', was the first IAF unit to fly the Tejas.

questions.q: What is a notable structural feature of the Tejas that helps keep its weight low?
options: Extensive use of carbon-fibre composite materials
questions.explanation: The Tejas makes extensive use of carbon-fibre composites, reducing weight and radar signature.

questions.q: Which control system does the Tejas rely on to remain stable in flight?
options: A digital fly-by-wire flight control system
questions.explanation: The Tejas uses a digital fly-by-wire system because its relaxed-stability design needs constant computer correction.

questions.q: A naval version of the Tejas was developed to operate from what platform?
questions.explanation: A carrier-capable naval variant of the Tejas was developed for the Indian Navy's aircraft carriers.

questions.q: Which of these is a role the multirole Tejas is designed to perform?
questions.explanation: As a multirole fighter, the Tejas can perform both air-to-air combat and air-to-ground strike missions.

questions.q: When did the Tejas first receive its Initial Operational Clearance (IOC), allowing limited service use?
questions.explanation: The Tejas achieved Initial Operational Clearance in January 2011.

questions.q: In which year did the Tejas achieve Final Operational Clearance (FOC) for the Indian Air Force?
questions.explanation: The Tejas Mk1 received Final Operational Clearance in early 2019.

questions.q: The more capable upgraded variant developed from the Tejas is designated which of the following?
questions.explanation: The Tejas Mk1A is an upgraded variant with improved radar, electronic warfare and more weapons.

questions.q: Which South American country did India actively court as a prospective export customer for the Tejas?
questions.explanation: India promoted the Tejas to Argentina as a prospective export buyer, though Argentina ultimately chose second-hand Danish F-16s instead.

questions.q: The first technology-demonstrator Tejas was given which designation?
questions.explanation: The first technology demonstrator that flew in 2001 was designated TD-1.

questions.q: An indigenous engine, intended (but not used in production Mk1) to power the Tejas, was named what?
questions.explanation: India's GTRE Kaveri engine was developed for the Tejas, but production aircraft use the GE F404 instead.

questions.q: Which advanced radar type is fitted to the upgraded Tejas Mk1A for improved detection?
questions.explanation: The Tejas Mk1A is equipped with an AESA radar, a significant capability upgrade over the original.

questions.q: Approximately what is the maximum speed of the Tejas, reflecting its supersonic capability?
questions.explanation: The Tejas is a supersonic fighter with a top speed of roughly Mach 1.6 to 1.8.
